prestazioni: Engine swap to a Toyota 2NZ-FE (1.3L) with a custom-built turbo kit featuring a Garrett GT2860RS turbocharger, HKS SSQV4 blow-off valve, and a Tomei 4-2-1 stainless steel exhaust manifold. Supporting mods include a GReddy intercooler, custom 3-inch turbo-back exhaust with a Fujitsubo Giken axle-back, and a Haltech Elite 1500 ECU for precise fuel and ignition mapping.

Suspension overhaul with Tein Flex Z coilovers (Vitz RS-specific valving), Cusco 22mm front and 20mm rear sway bars, and Whiteline adjustable rear camber arms. Polyurethane bushings from SuperPro (part numbers SPF0115-K and SPF0116-K) replace all OEM rubber bushings for improved handling response.

Drivetrain upgrades including a Cusco 1.5-way mechanical LSD (part number LSD952), TRD 75W-90 gear oil, and a custom lightweight flywheel paired with an Exedy Stage 2 clutch kit. The final drive ratio is swapped to a 4.312:1 gearset from a Toyota Echo RS for quicker acceleration.
estetica: Vertex Lang II widebody kit (Vitz RS-specific, part numbers VX-LANG2-FR, VX-LANG2-RR, and VX-LANG2-SK) with custom carbon fiber side skirts and rear diffuser from Seibon Carbon. The kit is paired with Work Emotion CR Kiwami wheels (15x7 +38 offset) wrapped in Yokohama Advan Neova AD08R tires (195/50R15).

Custom front bumper lip from TRD (part number PTR01-52060) modified with a carbon fiber splitter from APR Performance (Vitz RS-specific, part number CF-81102). Rear wing is a custom 3-piece carbon fiber unit from Voltex (Type 3, part number 1132-3000), mounted on custom aluminum pedestals.

Interior stripped and fitted with Bride Zeta III reclinable bucket seats (Vitz RS-specific mounts), a Nardi Classic 350mm steering wheel with a Works Bell quick-release hub, and a custom roll cage from AutoPower (6-point, bolt-in, part number AP-6006). Shift knob is a custom titanium unit from Buddy Club (part number BC-TI-SK).
